perturbation.original_text: This Agreement applies to any Confidential Information that may have been provided by either Party before or after the Effective Date, and will continue to govern all disclosures of Confidential Information, until terminated on thirty (30) days writ- ten notice by either Party to the other, except that each Party’s obligations relating to Confidential Information disclosed prior to termination will continue for so long as the Confidential Information remains confidential and proprietary.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement applies to any Confidential Information that may have been provided by either Party before or after the Effective Date, and will continue to govern all disclosures of Confidential Information, until terminated on thirty (30) days writ- ten notice by either Member to the other, except that each Party’s obligations relating to Confidential Information disclosed prior to termination will continue for so long as the Confidential Information remains confidential and proprietary.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Party' to 'Member' when discussing termination introduces inconsistency. 'Party' is defined as COMPANY X or COMPANY Y, while 'Member' is undefined. This creates confusion about who can terminate the agreement and if COMPANY X or COMPANY Y are consider Members.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term. This Agreement applies to any Confidential Information that may have been provided by either Party before or after the Effective Date, and will continue to govern all disclosures of Confidential Information, until terminated on thirty (30) days writ- ten notice by either Member to the other, except that each Party’s obligations relating to Confidential Information disclosed prior to termination will continue for so long as the Confidential Information remains confidential and proprietary.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Party' with 'Member' in the termination clause. 'Party' is a defined term (COMPANY X or COMPANY Y), while 'Member' is not defined. The contradicted text contains the same problematic change. The explanation highlights that this creates confusion about who can terminate the agreement, as it's unclear whether COMPANY X or COMPANY Y are considered 'Members'. This misalignment of terminology introduces a direct contradiction, making it unclear who has the authority to terminate, which aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party agrees that the wrongful disclosure of Confidential In- formation may cause irreparable injury that is inadequately compensable by monetary damages. As such, either Party may seek injunctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction for the breach and/or the threatened breach of this Agreement in addition to any other remedies it may seek at law or equity, and the Recipient will not raise the defense of an adequate remedy at law.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party agrees that the wrongful disclosure of Confidential In- formation may cause irreparable injury that is inadequately compensable by monetary damages. As such, either Individual may seek injunctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction for the breach and/or the threatened breach of this Agreement in addition to any other remedies it may seek at law or equity, and the Recipient will not raise the defense of an adequate remedy at law.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Party" to "Individual" creates ambiguity in Section 15. The agreement defines Party to mean COMPANY X and COMPANY Y. Changing it to "Individual" opens a loophole if an individual related to COMPANY X/Y breaches, but not COMPANY X/Y itself, as it isn't clear if Individual means a person connected to either company or another entity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Injunctive Relief. Each Party agrees that the wrongful disclosure of Confidential In- formation may cause irreparable injury that is inadequately compensable by monetary damages. As such, either Individual may seek injunctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction for the breach and/or the threatened breach of this Agreement in addition to any other remedies it may seek at law or equity, and the Recipient will not raise the defense of an adequate remedy at law.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Party', which is defined as COMPANY X and COMPANY Y. The changed text replaces 'Party' with 'Individual'. The contradicted text repeats the changed text which now says 'Individual'. According to the explanation, this creates ambiguity and a potential loophole because 'Individual' is not defined. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the term 'Individual' is now used in a legally binding context without clarity, potentially conflicting with the intent of the agreement and creating uncertainty about who is subject to the injunctive relief.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party shall fully indemnify the other against any and all ac- tions, claims, liability, costs, damages, charges and expenses suffered or incurred in connection with or arising out of any breach by a Party of any of the provisions of this Agreement, or by any unauthorized disclosure or use of Confidential Information by a third party or by any employee of any Party to whom Confidential Information has been disclosed or who has been allowed access thereto.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party shall fully indemnify the other against any and all ac- tions, claims, liability, costs, damages, charges and expenses suffered or incurred in connection with or arising out of any breach by a Supplier of any of the provisions of this Agreement, or by any unauthorized disclosure or use of Confidential Information by a third party or by any employee of any Party to whom Confidential Information has been disclosed or who has been allowed access thereto.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Party' with 'Supplier' in the context of indemnification in Section 16 leads to potential issues. 'Party' is previously defined as COMPANY X and COMPANY Y. 'Supplier' has not been defined. It introduces confusion because there may be multiple suppliers for COMPANY X or Y, so its unclear if COMPANY X/Y will indemnify each other or some unknown Supplier.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Indemnification. Each Party shall fully indemnify the other against any and all ac- tions, claims, liability, costs, damages, charges and expenses suffered or incurred in connection with or arising out of any breach by a Supplier of any of the provisions of this Agreement, or by any unauthorized disclosure or use of Confidential Information by a third party or by any employee of any Party to whom Confidential Information has been disclosed or who has been allowed access thereto. Each Party acknowledges and confirms that a breach of its obligations hereunder cannot be compensated adequately solely by an award of damages or indemnity or other pecuniary remedy, and that the non-breaching Party shall also be entitled in the event of any such breach to the rem-edies of injunction, specific performance, and/or other equitable relief in respect of any such breach. Nothing herein shall be construed as a waiver by either Party of any of its rights including rights to damages or indemnity or other pecuniary remedy.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that each 'Party' will indemnify the other. 'Party' is defined. The changed text replaces 'Party' with 'Supplier'. 'Supplier' is not defined. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. Since 'Supplier' is undefined, it creates ambiguity and a potential conflict because the 'Party' (COMPANY X or Y) may have multiple suppliers, making it unclear which supplier the indemnification applies to. The change introduces ambiguity about which entity is responsible for indemnification, leading to an in-text contradiction and uncertainty in the contract's obligations because 'Supplier' may not be a 'Party'.

perturbation.original_text: Further, the Parties agree that the Recipient shall use any and all Confidential Information received from the Discloser solely for the Purpose and no other use may be made without the Discloser’s prior written consent, which shall be granted or with- held in its sole discretion.
perturbation.changed_text: Further, the Signatories agree that the Recipient shall use any and all Confidential Information received from the Discloser solely for the Purpose and no other use may be made without the Discloser’s prior written consent, which shall be granted or with- held in its sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: Changing Parties to Signatories in Section 7 creates a contradiction. Parties is defined at the beginning of the contract. Signatories is undefined, leading to ambiguity about who is bound to the conditions listed. The original "Parties agree" is no longer clear in its enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Transfer. All Confidential Information disclosed by any Party shall remain the property of the Discloser. This Agreement does not confer any license, right, interest, or title in or to Confidential Information other than as expressly set forth in this Agree- ment. Further, the Signatories agree that the Recipient shall use any and all Confidential Information received from the Discloser solely for the Purpose and no other use may be made without the Discloser’s prior written consent, which shall be granted or with- held in its sole discretion.
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'Parties' agree, which aligns with the definition of 'Parties' at the beginning of the contract. The changed text replaces 'Parties' with 'Signatories', which is undefined in the contract. The contradicted text also uses 'Signatories'. Because the term is undefined and the 'Parties' is a defined term, it introduces ambiguity about who is bound by the agreement regarding confidential information, creating a contradiction because it's unclear who the obligation to maintain confidentiality applies to.
